articles.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission: Implementation Roadmap Unveiled, India Aims for Global Leadership
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) today released the detailed implementation roadmap for the National Green Hydrogen Mission, following its significant allocation in the recent Union Budget.
• The roadmap outlines strategies for domestic production, demand creation, export opportunities, and R&D in green hydrogen technologies.
• Key focus areas include developing green hydrogen production hubs, establishing a robust ecosystem for electrolyser manufacturing, and promoting pilot projects in steel, refining, and fertilizer sectors.
• Financial incentives, including production-linked incentives (PLI) and viability gap funding (VGF), are detailed to attract private investment and reduce production costs.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing significantly to energy security and decarbonization goals.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Economic Growth and Development
Environmental Pollution and Degradation
Science and Technology- Developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched by MNRE.
Aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production.
Focus on reducing carbon emissions and achieving energy independence.
Key components include PLI scheme for electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production.
Target sectors for initial adoption: refining, fertilizer, steel.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the National Green Hydrogen Mission for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the economic potential and challenges in establishing India as a global green hydrogen hub.
Evaluate the role of government policies, including PLI and VGF, in fostering green hydrogen ecosystem.
Examine the interlinkages between the Green Hydrogen Mission and India's commitment to net-zero emissions by 2070.
Critically assess the technological readiness and infrastructure requirements for widespread adoption of green hydrogen.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting green hydrogen production facilities with solar panels and wind turbines in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

articles.headline: Supreme Court to Hear Petitions Challenging Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today announced it would commence hearings on a batch of petitions challenging various provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• Petitioners, including privacy advocates and civil society organizations, have raised concerns regarding the Act's potential impact on fundamental right to privacy, state surveillance, and autonomy of data fiduciaries.
• Key contentious points include the broad exemptions granted to government agencies, the composition and powers of the Data Protection Board, and the definition of 'personal data' and 'significant data fiduciaries'.
• The government is expected to defend the Act, emphasizing its role in safeguarding citizen data while balancing national security and public order interests.
• The outcome of these hearings will have significant implications for data governance, digital rights, and the balance between state power and individual privacy in India's rapidly expanding digital economy.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
Vulnerability of various sections of society to cybercrime.
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: DPDP Act, 2023, aims to regulate processing of digital personal data.
Key bodies: Data Protection Board of India.
Concerns raised: exemptions for government, composition of DPB.
Based on the recommendations of the Justice B.N. Srikrishna Committee.
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, in light of the Right to Privacy judgment.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities presented by the DPDP Act for India's digital economy and data governance framework.
Critically evaluate the balance struck by the Act between individual privacy rights and state interests, particularly concerning exemptions.
Examine the role of the Data Protection Board and its potential effectiveness in enforcing the provisions of the Act.
Suggest reforms or amendments that could strengthen the DPDP Act to better protect citizen data and privacy.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on a law book, symbolizing judicial proceedings, with digital data streams in the background.
articles.trivia: The 'Right to Privacy' was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court in the landmark K.S. Puttaswamy judgment in 2017.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes: Focus on Maritime Security and Trade Connectivity in Indo-Pacific
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded today with leaders emphasizing enhanced cooperation in maritime security, trade, and digital connectivity across the Indo-Pacific region.
• A joint statement highlighted commitments to uphold a rules-based international order, freedom of navigation, and peaceful resolution of disputes, particularly in the South China Sea.
• Discussions focused on accelerating the implementation of the ASEAN-India Free Trade Area (AIFTA) and exploring new avenues for supply chain resilience and digital economy integration.
• India reiterated its 'Act East' policy and its vision for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, aligning with ASEAN's Outlook on the Indo-Pacific (AOIP).
• New initiatives were announced in areas of capacity building, counter-terrorism, and cultural exchanges, aimed at deepening strategic partnership.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.

India is a Dialogue Partner of ASEAN.
ASEAN-India Free Trade Area (AIFTA) is in effect.
India's 'Act East' policy focuses on strengthening ties with Southeast Asian nations.
ASEAN comprises 10 member states.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India-ASEAN strategic partnership for regional stability and economic integration in the Indo-Pacific.
Discuss how India's 'Act East' policy aligns with ASEAN's vision for a free and open Indo-Pacific.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in enhancing trade and maritime security cooperation between India and ASEAN member states.
Examine the role of multilateral forums like the India-ASEAN Summit in addressing common challenges such as climate change and terrorism.
Suggest measures to further deepen India-ASEAN ties in emerging areas like digital economy and blue economy.
articles.imageDescription: Leaders from India and ASEAN member states shaking hands and posing for a group photograph.
articles.trivia: ASEAN was established on August 8, 1967, in Bangkok, Thailand, with the signing of the ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration).

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Inflation Concerns, Signals Cautious Optimism for Growth
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today announced its decision to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive meeting.
• The decision reflects ongoing concerns about persistent retail inflation, particularly in food prices, despite signs of moderation in core inflation.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das emphasized the need for continued vigilance to ensure inflation aligns with the target of 4%, while also acknowledging robust economic growth.
• The MPC maintained its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance, indicating that liquidity management would remain tight to anchor inflationary expectations.
• Experts anticipate the RBI will continue to monitor global commodity prices, monsoon performance, and fiscal policy measures before considering any rate cuts later in the year.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy and fiscal policy.
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets the policy rates.
Primary objective of MPC is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Inflation target for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its implications for inflation and economic growth.
Discuss the challenges faced by the Monetary Policy Committee in balancing inflation control with supporting economic activity.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance in managing liquidity and inflationary pressures.
Examine the external and internal factors influencing RBI's monetary policy decisions, including global commodity prices and domestic demand.
Critically assess the coordination between monetary and fiscal policy in achieving India's macroeconomic stability goals.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building with its logo prominently displayed.
articles.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, in accordance with the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: Project Tiger Completes 50 Years: New Conservation Strategies for Big Cats Unveiled
articles.explainer:
• Celebrating 50 years of Project Tiger, the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) today announced new strategies for tiger conservation and broader big cat protection.
• The new approach emphasizes community involvement, use of advanced technology for surveillance, and strengthening anti-poaching measures across tiger reserves.
• Focus will also be on habitat restoration, managing human-wildlife conflict, and creating corridors for tiger movement to ensure genetic diversity.
• The initiative will extend its scope to other big cats like leopards and snow leopards, recognizing their ecological importance and shared threats.
• India's tiger population has significantly increased since Project Tiger's inception in 1973, positioning the country as a global leader in tiger conservation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
prelimsPoints: Project Tiger was launched in 1973.
National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) is the statutory body for tiger conservation.
India is home to over 70% of the world's tiger population.
Tiger is listed in Schedule I of the Wildlife Protection Act, 1972.
Global Tiger Day is celebrated on July 29.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the success of Project Tiger over the past 50 years in conserving tiger populations and their habitats in India.
Discuss the new strategies announced for big cat conservation, including community involvement and technological interventions.
Analyze the challenges posed by human-wildlife conflict and habitat fragmentation to tiger conservation efforts.
Examine the role of international cooperation and national policies in achieving conservation goals for endangered species.
Suggest comprehensive measures to ensure the long-term sustainability of big cat populations in India amidst developmental pressures.
articles.imageDescription: A majestic Bengal tiger walking through dense forest foliage in a tiger reserve.
articles.trivia: India's tiger population has more than quadrupled since the launch of Project Tiger, making it one of the most successful conservation programs globally.

articles.headline: ISRO Prepares for Venus Orbiter Mission 'Shukrayaan-1' Launch Window
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today confirmed that preparations are in full swing for the launch of its ambitious Venus Orbiter Mission, 'Shukrayaan-1', targeting a launch window in late 2026 or early 2027.
• Shukrayaan-1 aims to study Venus's atmosphere, surface, and subsurface, providing crucial insights into the planet's geological and atmospheric evolution.
• The mission payload includes a high-resolution synthetic aperture radar (SAR) to map the Venusian surface through its thick clouds, and instruments to study atmospheric composition and dynamics.
• This mission marks India's first foray into exploring Venus, building on the success of Chandrayaan and Mangalyaan missions.
• International collaborations are also being explored for data sharing and scientific analysis, enhancing global understanding of Earth's 'sister planet'.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: Shukrayaan-1 is India's first mission to Venus.
ISRO is the primary agency for the mission.
Aims to study Venus's atmosphere and surface.
Payloads include SAR for surface mapping.
Venus is Earth's 'sister planet' due to similar size and mass.
mainsPoints: Discuss the scientific objectives and potential discoveries of ISRO's Shukrayaan-1 mission to Venus.
Analyze the significance of India's planetary exploration efforts for its scientific capabilities and global standing.
Examine the technological challenges involved in designing and executing a mission to Venus, given its extreme environment.
Evaluate the benefits of international collaboration in space exploration, citing examples from past missions.
How do missions like Shukrayaan-1 contribute to our understanding of Earth's climate and future habitability?
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of the Shukrayaan-1 orbiter approaching Venus, with its scientific instruments deployed.
articles.trivia: Venus is the hottest planet in our solar system, even though Mercury is closer to the Sun, due to its thick atmosphere of greenhouse gases.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Progress Report on Foundational Literacy and Numeracy Released
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education today released a comprehensive progress report on the implementation of foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) goals under the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report highlights significant strides in training teachers, developing curriculum frameworks, and distributing learning materials across states, particularly under the NIPUN Bharat Mission.
• Challenges persist in remote areas and among socio-economically disadvantaged groups, requiring targeted interventions and increased resource allocation.
• The government plans to intensify efforts to achieve universal foundational literacy and numeracy by 2027, a key objective of the NEP.
• Emphasis is being placed on leveraging technology for blended learning, continuous assessment, and capacity building for educators.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry —the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.

NIPUN Bharat Mission is a flagship program under NEP for FLN.

Ministry of Education is the nodal ministry.
Emphasizes mother tongue as medium of instruction in early grades.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives and key features of the National Education Policy 2020 concerning Foundational Literacy and Numeracy.
Discuss the progress made and challenges encountered in implementing FLN goals across diverse regions of India.
Evaluate the role of the NIPUN Bharat Mission in achieving universal foundational learning outcomes for young children.
Examine how technological interventions and teacher capacity building are being utilized to enhance FLN delivery.
Suggest policy recommendations to address disparities in educational access and quality, particularly for vulnerable groups, under NEP 2020.
articles.imageDescription: Children engaged in a classroom setting, learning with colorful educational materials.
articles.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the third such policy in India's history, following those in 1968 and 1986.

articles.headline: India Enhances Border Infrastructure along Northern Frontier with Advanced Surveillance Systems
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence today announced significant advancements in border infrastructure development and deployment of advanced surveillance systems along India's northern frontier.
• The upgrade includes construction of new roads, bridges, and tunnels to ensure all-weather connectivity for troops and equipment in challenging terrains.
• Integrated surveillance systems, comprising drones, satellite imagery, and ground-based sensors, are being operationalized to enhance situational awareness and deter infiltration.
• This strategic push aims to bolster India's defensive capabilities and ensure rapid response to any security challenges in the region.
• The move is part of a broader strategy to modernize border management and integrate technology for effective monitoring and protection of national borders.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: India shares land borders with seven countries.
Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is responsible for building and maintaining border roads.
Use of drones and satellite imagery for border surveillance.
Integrated border management aims for comprehensive security.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of enhancing border infrastructure and surveillance along India's northern frontier.
Analyze how technological advancements, such as drones and satellite imagery, contribute to effective border management.
Examine the challenges faced in constructing infrastructure in high-altitude and rugged terrains.
Evaluate the impact of improved border infrastructure on national security and regional stability.
Suggest further measures for modernizing India's border management strategy to address evolving security threats.
articles.imageDescription: Military personnel patrolling a newly constructed road in a mountainous border region, with surveillance equipment in the background.
articles.trivia: The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) was formed in 1960 and has been instrumental in developing and maintaining road networks in India's border areas.

articles.headline: Election Commission of India Announces Preparations for Upcoming Assembly Elections: Focus on Digital Voter Services
articles.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) today held a press conference detailing its preparedness for upcoming Assembly Elections in several states later this year.
• A key focus area is the expansion and enhancement of digital voter services, including online voter registration, correction facilities, and accessible information dissemination through dedicated apps and portals.
• The ECI emphasized measures to ensure free, fair, and transparent elections, including strict enforcement of the Model Code of Conduct and deployment of central forces.
• Special arrangements are being planned to facilitate voting for persons with disabilities (PwDs) and senior citizens, ensuring inclusive participation.
• Training programs for election officials and awareness campaigns for voters are already underway, leveraging technology for wider reach.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
prelimsPoints: ECI is a constitutional body (Article 324).
Responsible for conducting elections to Parliament, State Legislatures, President, and Vice President.
Model Code of Conduct is a set of guidelines for political parties and candidates.
EVMs and VVPATs are used in elections.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and powers of the Election Commission of India in ensuring free and fair elections.
Analyze the impact of digital voter services and technological interventions on electoral transparency and accessibility.
Examine the challenges faced by the ECI in conducting elections, including issues of misinformation and electoral malpractices.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the Model Code of Conduct in regulating political behavior during election periods.
Suggest reforms to further strengthen the electoral process in India, promoting greater participation and integrity.
articles.imageDescription: The logo of the Election Commission of India displayed on a digital screen, with a voter's hand interacting with a digital device.
articles.trivia: India held its first general election in 1951-52, marking a monumental exercise in democracy with over 173 million eligible voters.

articles.headline: Government Launches New PLI Scheme for Advanced Battery Manufacturing to Boost EV Ecosystem
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Heavy Industries today announced the launch of a new Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme specifically targeting Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) battery manufacturing in India.
• The scheme aims to attract significant domestic and foreign investment, reduce import dependence, and establish India as a global manufacturing hub for EV batteries.
• Incentives will be provided to manufacturers based on their output and domestic value addition, encouraging local production of critical components and raw materials.
• This initiative is crucial for accelerating the adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) and achieving India's ambitious climate goals.
• The scheme is expected to foster R&D in battery technology, create employment opportunities, and enhance India's energy storage capabilities.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy.
Science and Technology- Developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: PLI scheme aims to boost domestic manufacturing in various sectors.
ACC battery manufacturing is crucial for the EV industry.
Ministry of Heavy Industries is the nodal ministry for this PLI scheme.
Objective: reduce import dependence and promote self-reliance.
India is promoting FAME-II scheme for EV adoption.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the new PLI scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell battery manufacturing in accelerating India's EV transition.
Discuss how PLI schemes contribute to 'Make in India' initiative and enhance India's manufacturing competitiveness in critical sectors.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in building a robust domestic supply chain for EV battery components and raw materials.
Evaluate the potential impact of this scheme on job creation, technological innovation, and India's energy security.
Critically assess the role of government incentives in fostering green mobility and achieving climate change targets.
articles.imageDescription: A schematic diagram of an Advanced Chemistry Cell battery pack with 'Made in India' label, alongside an electric car.
articles.trivia: The global demand for electric vehicle batteries is projected to grow significantly, with lithium-ion batteries currently dominating the market.

articles.headline: Himalayan Ecosystems Under Threat: Report Highlights Glacial Retreat and Biodiversity Loss
articles.explainer:
• A new report released by the National Institute of Himalayan Environment (NIHE) today warned of severe threats to Himalayan ecosystems, citing accelerated glacial retreat and significant biodiversity loss.
• The report attributes these changes primarily to climate change, leading to altered precipitation patterns, increased extreme weather events, and upward migration of species.
• Consequences include increased risks of flash floods, landslides, water scarcity for downstream populations, and irreversible loss of unique flora and fauna.
• The study calls for urgent, integrated strategies for climate adaptation, sustainable resource management, and enhanced transboundary cooperation.
• Recommendations include early warning systems for natural disasters, promotion of climate-resilient agriculture, and strengthening protected area networks.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Himalayas are one of the youngest and highest mountain ranges.
Known as the 'Water Tower of Asia' due to vast glaciers.
Home to a rich biodiversity, including several endemic species.
Climate change leads to glacial melt and changes in snowfall patterns.
NIHE (National Institute of Himalayan Environment) is a research organization.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multifaceted impacts of climate change on the Himalayan ecosystems, focusing on glacial retreat and biodiversity loss.
Discuss the socio-economic consequences of these environmental changes for communities residing in the Himalayas and downstream regions.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current national and international strategies for climate adaptation and disaster risk reduction in the Himalayan region.
Examine the challenges in implementing sustainable development practices and conservation efforts in fragile mountain environments.
Suggest a comprehensive policy framework for the protection and resilience of the Himalayan ecosystems, including transboundary cooperation.
articles.imageDescription: A panoramic view of a retreating glacier in the Himalayas, with visible signs of melting and rock debris.
articles.trivia: The Himalayas stretch across five countries: India, Nepal, Bhutan, China, and Pakistan, and influence the climate of the Indian subcontinent.

articles.headline: India Pledges Strong Support for Global South Development Initiatives at UN Forum
articles.explainer:
• India today reiterated its strong commitment to supporting development initiatives for the Global South at a high-level United Nations forum on sustainable development.
• External Affairs Minister emphasized India's role as a voice for developing nations and highlighted its contributions through capacity building, technology transfer, and concessional financing.
• Discussions focused on addressing common challenges such as climate finance, food security, debt sustainability, and achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
• India called for greater equity in global governance structures and stressed the importance of South-South cooperation as a complement to North-South development partnerships.
• The announcement reinforces India's growing influence and leadership in advocating for the collective interests of developing countries on the global stage.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: Global South refers to countries in Asia, Africa, and Latin America.
Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) are 17 goals set by the UN.
India is a prominent advocate for the Global South.
South-South Cooperation involves cooperation among developing countries.
UN forums discuss global issues like climate change and poverty.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's evolving role and leadership in championing the interests of the Global South at international forums.
Discuss the significance of South-South cooperation in achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and addressing global challenges.
Examine the challenges faced by the Global South, including climate finance, debt burden, and equitable access to technology.
Evaluate India's contributions to capacity building and development assistance in various developing countries.
Critically assess the need for reforms in global governance structures to ensure greater representation and voice for the Global South.
articles.imageDescription: A world map highlighting countries of the Global South, with the UN logo in the foreground.
articles.trivia: The term 'Global South' gained prominence after the end of the Cold War, replacing earlier terms like 'Third World'.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Crosses Major Milestone with Health Records Digitization
articles.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) today announced a significant milestone, having successfully linked over 500 million health records to Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA).
• This achievement marks a major step towards creating a seamless online platform for healthcare services, enabling citizens to access and share their health records digitally.
• The mission aims to establish a national digital health ecosystem that supports universal health coverage and improves efficiency in healthcare delivery.
• Key components include ABHA ID generation, Health Professional Registry, Health Facility Registry, and a consent-based framework for data sharing.
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare emphasizes the importance of data privacy and security in the digital health infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: ABDM was launched in 2021 by the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare.
Aims to create a national digital health ecosystem.
ABHA ID is a unique health identifier for citizens.
It is implemented by the National Health Authority (NHA).
Focuses on consent-based data sharing.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives and key components of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission in transforming India's healthcare landscape.
Discuss the significance of linking a large number of health records to ABHA IDs for universal health coverage and efficient service delivery.
Examine the opportunities and challenges in implementing a nationwide digital health infrastructure, including data privacy and security concerns.
Evaluate the potential impact of ABDM on improving healthcare access, reducing costs, and enhancing patient outcomes.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access to digital health services and address the digital divide in healthcare.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing the ABHA logo and various digital health services connecting to form a network.
articles.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PMJAY) is the world's largest government-funded healthcare program.

articles.headline: Women's Reservation Bill: State Assemblies Begin Deliberations on Implementation Modalities
articles.explainer:
• Following the parliamentary passage of the Women's Reservation Bill (Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam), several state assemblies have commenced deliberations on the modalities of its implementation.
• The bill mandates 33% reservation for women in the Lok Sabha and state legislative assemblies, to be implemented after the next delimitation exercise.
• States are discussing the process of delimitation, potential changes to constituency boundaries, and strategies to ensure fair representation and political empowerment of women.
• Political parties are also strategizing on candidate selection and fostering a more inclusive political environment at the grassroots level.
• The implementation of this landmark legislation is expected to significantly alter India's political landscape, promoting greater gender parity in legislative bodies.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: The Bill provides for 33% reservation for women in Lok Sabha and State Assemblies.
Implementation is linked to the next delimitation exercise and census.
Constitutional Amendment was required for its passage.
It was passed as the 128th Constitutional Amendment Bill, becoming the 106th Constitutional Amendment Act.
Reservation will rotate among different constituencies.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical context and significance of the Women's Reservation Bill (Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam) in India's democratic journey.
Discuss the challenges and complexities involved in the implementation of the bill, particularly concerning delimitation and constituency rotation.
Evaluate the potential impact of 33% reservation for women on the quality of governance, policy-making, and political culture in India.
Examine how this legislation addresses the issue of gender disparity in political representation and promotes women's empowerment.
Suggest complementary measures beyond reservation that can ensure meaningful participation and leadership of women in politics.
articles.imageDescription: Women parliamentarians celebrating inside the Parliament House, symbolizing the passage of the Women's Reservation Bill.
articles.trivia: The Women's Reservation Bill was first introduced in 1996 but faced multiple hurdles before its eventual passage in 2023.

articles.headline: High Courts Urged to Expedite Cases Related to Environmental Violations and Pollution
articles.explainer:
• The Chief Justice of India (CJI) today urged High Courts across the country to prioritize and expedite the disposal of cases related to environmental violations and pollution.
• The CJI highlighted the critical need for timely judicial intervention to protect natural resources, enforce environmental laws, and hold polluters accountable.
• Concerns were raised about the backlog of environmental cases, which often leads to irreversible damage before legal remedies can be effectively implemented.
• The call emphasizes the judiciary's role in upholding the 'polluter pays' principle and the 'precautionary principle' in environmental governance.
• Training for judicial officers on complex environmental laws and scientific evidence was also recommended to improve judicial efficiency in this specialized domain.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary Ministries and Departments of the Government; pressure groups and formal/informal associations and their role in the Polity.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: National Green Tribunal (NGT) was established in 2010 for effective and expeditious disposal of cases relating to environmental protection.
'Polluter Pays Principle' states that the party responsible for pollution should pay for the damage caused.
'Precautionary Principle' advises taking preventive action in the face of uncertainty.
Article 21 (Right to Life) includes the right to a clean environment.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of the Indian judiciary, particularly the High Courts, in enforcing environmental laws and promoting environmental justice.
Discuss the challenges faced by the judicial system in expeditiously disposing of environmental cases, including technical complexities and backlog.
Evaluate the effectiveness of legal principles like 'polluter pays' and 'precautionary principle' in achieving environmental protection.
Examine the need for specialized training for judicial officers to handle complex environmental litigation.
Suggest reforms to strengthen environmental governance and ensure timely redressal of environmental grievances in India.
articles.imageDescription: A courtroom scene with scales of justice overlaid on a background depicting nature and pollution, symbolizing environmental justice.
articles.trivia: India was one of the first countries to establish an environmental court, the National Green Tribunal, in 2010.

articles.headline: Government to Boost Millets Production and Export under 'Shree Anna' Initiative
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare today announced enhanced measures to boost the production and export of millets, branded as 'Shree Anna', following the emphasis in the recent Union Budget.
• The initiative aims to promote millets as a superfood, recognizing their nutritional benefits and resilience to climate change, especially for small and marginal farmers.
• Plans include providing financial assistance to farmers for millet cultivation, improving post-harvest infrastructure, and supporting value addition and branding efforts.
• India, being a major producer of millets, seeks to become a global hub for 'Shree Anna' through aggressive marketing and export promotion.
• This push aligns with the International Year of Millets (2023) and India's commitment to food security and sustainable agriculture.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: Millets are a collective term for small-seeded grasses.
India is the largest producer of millets globally.
2023 was declared as the International Year of Millets (IYM) by the UN.
Millets are known for their nutritional value (rich in fiber, protein, minerals).
Examples: Jowar, Bajra, Ragi.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of millets ('Shree Anna') for India's food security, nutritional well-being, and agricultural sustainability.
Discuss the government's initiatives to boost millet production and export, including financial assistance and infrastructure development.
Examine the role of millets in climate-resilient agriculture and their potential to address challenges posed by climate change.
Evaluate the economic benefits for small and marginal farmers from increased millet cultivation and value addition.
Suggest strategies for popularizing millets domestically and internationally, positioning India as a global leader in 'Shree Anna' promotion.
articles.imageDescription: Various types of millets (Jowar, Bajra, Ragi) displayed in baskets, with a farmer in the background.
articles.trivia: Millets are ancient grains, cultivated for thousands of years, and are known for their ability to grow in arid and semi-arid regions with minimal water.

articles.headline: New Guidelines for Telemedicine Services Released to Expand Healthcare Access in Remote Areas
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare today issued updated guidelines for telemedicine services, aiming to further expand healthcare access, especially in remote and underserved areas.
• The revised framework simplifies regulations for registered medical practitioners, encourages the use of digital platforms, and clarifies ethical considerations for virtual consultations.
• Emphasis is placed on ensuring quality of care, patient safety, and data privacy while leveraging technology to bridge geographical barriers to healthcare.
• The guidelines also promote the integration of telemedicine with the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) to create a comprehensive digital health ecosystem.
• This move is expected to significantly benefit rural populations, reduce the burden on urban healthcare facilities, and enhance the overall efficiency of the health system.

Discuss the key features of the new telemedicine guidelines and their potential impact on healthcare delivery and patient safety.

articles.headline: India's Tourism Sector Sees Robust Growth Post-Pandemic, Focus on Sustainable and Experiential Tourism
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism today released data indicating a robust recovery and growth in India's tourism sector, surpassing pre-pandemic levels in both domestic and international arrivals.
• The resurgence is attributed to government initiatives like 'Dekho Apna Desh' and 'Swadesh Darshan', coupled with improved infrastructure and enhanced connectivity.
• A new strategy focuses on promoting sustainable tourism practices, responsible travel, and developing unique experiential tourism products, including eco-tourism and cultural circuits.
• Efforts are underway to leverage digital platforms for promotion and booking, enhancing the visitor experience and supporting local economies.
• The sector is poised for continued growth, contributing significantly to employment generation and foreign exchange earnings.

articles.headline: New Species of Fauna Discovered in Western Ghats, Highlights Biodiversity Richness and Conservation Needs
articles.explainer:
• Scientists from the Zoological Survey of India (ZSI) today announced the discovery of several new species of amphibians and insects in the biodiverse Western Ghats region.
• The findings underscore the immense biodiversity richness of the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the world's eight 'hottest' biodiversity hotspots.
• These discoveries highlight the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts, protection of critical habitats, and sustainable development practices in the region.
• Researchers emphasized the importance of continued exploration and documentation of species to better understand and preserve India's natural heritage.
• The new species will undergo detailed taxonomic classification and genetic analysis, contributing valuable data to global biodiversity inventories.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.

They are a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
ZSI (Zoological Survey of India) is responsible for surveying faunal resources.
Biodiversity hotspots are regions with high levels of endemic species facing significant habitat loss.
mainsPoints: Analyze the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces from human activities and climate change.
Discuss the importance of discovering new species for understanding biodiversity and guiding conservation strategies.
Examine the role of institutions like the Zoological Survey of India in documenting and preserving India's faunal diversity.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing effective conservation measures in a region with high population density and developmental pressures.
Suggest integrated approaches for sustainable development and biodiversity conservation in the Western Ghats region.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up photograph of a newly discovered colorful amphibian species found in the Western Ghats rainforest.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas, and their forests influence the Indian monsoon weather patterns.

articles.headline: Government to Launch New Scheme for Skill Development in Emerging Technologies for Youth Empowerment
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship today announced plans to launch a new scheme aimed at upskilling and reskilling India's youth in emerging technologies.
• The initiative will focus on areas such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine Learning (ML), Data Science, Robotics, and Blockchain, aligning with industry demands.
• Partnerships with industry leaders, educational institutions, and international organizations will be forged to develop relevant curricula and provide hands-on training.
• The scheme seeks to address the skill gap in the rapidly evolving digital economy, enhance employability, and foster an innovation ecosystem.
• Special provisions will be made for vocational training and certification, ensuring that a broad spectrum of youth, including those from economically weaker sections, can benefit.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
2. The mission is implemented by the Ministry of Power.
3. Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes are a key component to boost domestic manufacturing of electrolysers.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export. Statement 2 is incorrect. The mission is primarily implemented by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE). Statement 3 is correct. PLI schemes are indeed a key component to incentivize domestic manufacturing of electrolysers and green hydrogen production.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is a constitutional body responsible for conducting elections to the Parliament and State Legislatures in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Election Commission of India (ECI) is a constitutional body established under Article 324 of the Indian Constitution, responsible for conducting elections to the Parliament, State Legislatures, and the offices of the President and Vice President. NITI Aayog is an advisory body. UPSC is a constitutional body for recruitment. NHRC is a statutory body for human rights.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023:
1. It provides for the establishment of a Data Protection Board of India.
2. The Act grants complete exemption to all government agencies from its provisions.
3. The 'Right to be Forgotten' is a key provision under this Act.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The DPDP Act, 2023, provides for the establishment of a Data Protection Board of India. Statement 2 is incorrect. While the Act provides for exemptions for government agencies under certain conditions, it does not grant complete exemption to all. These exemptions have been a point of contention. Statement 3 is correct. The 'Right to Erasure' (often referred to as 'Right to be Forgotten') is a key right granted to Data Principals under the Act.

prelimsMocks.question: Project Tiger, a major wildlife conservation initiative in India, was launched in which year?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Project Tiger, India's flagship tiger conservation program, was launched on April 1, 1973, to protect the Bengal tiger and its habitats.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the Western Ghats is/are correct?
1. It is recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest' biodiversity hotspots.
2. It is older than the Himalayan mountain range.
3. It is entirely located within the state of Kerala.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The Western Ghats is indeed one of the world's eight 'hottest' biodiversity hotspots. Statement 2 is correct. Geologically, the Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas. Statement 3 is incorrect. The Western Ghats stretch across six states: Gujarat, Maharashtra, Goa, Karnataka, Kerala, and Tamil Nadu.

GS1.headline: Himalayan Ecosystems Under Threat: Report Highlights Glacial Retreat and Biodiversity Loss
GS1.explainer:
• A new report released by the National Institute of Himalayan Environment (NIHE) today warned of severe threats to Himalayan ecosystems, citing accelerated glacial retreat and significant biodiversity loss.
• The report attributes these changes primarily to climate change, leading to altered precipitation patterns, increased extreme weather events, and upward migration of species.
• Consequences include increased risks of flash floods, landslides, water scarcity for downstream populations, and irreversible loss of unique flora and fauna.
• The study calls for urgent, integrated strategies for climate adaptation, sustainable resource management, and enhanced transboundary cooperation.
• Recommendations include early warning systems for natural disasters, promotion of climate-resilient agriculture, and strengthening protected area networks.

prelimsPoints: Himalayas are one of the youngest and highest mountain ranges.
Known as the 'Water Tower of Asia' due to vast glaciers.
Home to a rich biodiversity, including several endemic species.
Climate change leads to glacial melt and changes in snowfall patterns.
NIHE (National Institute of Himalayan Environment) is a research organization.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multifaceted impacts of climate change on the Himalayan ecosystems, focusing on glacial retreat and biodiversity loss.
Discuss the socio-economic consequences of these environmental changes for communities residing in the Himalayas and downstream regions.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current national and international strategies for climate adaptation and disaster risk reduction in the Himalayan region.
Examine the challenges in implementing sustainable development practices and conservation efforts in fragile mountain environments.
Suggest a comprehensive policy framework for the protection and resilience of the Himalayan ecosystems, including transboundary cooperation.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court to Hear Petitions Challenging Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today announced it would commence hearings on a batch of petitions challenging various provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• Petitioners, including privacy advocates and civil society organizations, have raised concerns regarding the Act's potential impact on fundamental right to privacy, state surveillance, and autonomy of data fiduciaries.
• Key contentious points include the broad exemptions granted to government agencies, the composition and powers of the Data Protection Board, and the definition of 'personal data' and 'significant data fiduciaries'.
• The government is expected to defend the Act, emphasizing its role in safeguarding citizen data while balancing national security and public order interests.
• The outcome of these hearings will have significant implications for data governance, digital rights, and the balance between state power and individual privacy in India's rapidly expanding digital economy.

Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
Vulnerability of various sections of society to cybercrime.
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: DPDP Act, 2023, aims to regulate processing of digital personal data.
Key bodies: Data Protection Board of India.
Concerns raised: exemptions for government, composition of DPB.
Based on the recommendations of the Justice B.N. Srikrishna Committee.
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, in light of the Right to Privacy judgment.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities presented by the DPDP Act for India's digital economy and data governance framework.
Critically evaluate the balance struck by the Act between individual privacy rights and state interests, particularly concerning exemptions.
Examine the role of the Data Protection Board and its potential effectiveness in enforcing the provisions of the Act.
Suggest reforms or amendments that could strengthen the DPDP Act to better protect citizen data and privacy.

GS2.trivia: The 'Right to Privacy' was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court in the landmark K.S. Puttaswamy judgment in 2017.

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes: Focus on Maritime Security and Trade Connectivity in Indo-Pacific
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded today with leaders emphasizing enhanced cooperation in maritime security, trade, and digital connectivity across the Indo-Pacific region.
• A joint statement highlighted commitments to uphold a rules-based international order, freedom of navigation, and peaceful resolution of disputes, particularly in the South China Sea.
• Discussions focused on accelerating the implementation of the ASEAN-India Free Trade Area (AIFTA) and exploring new avenues for supply chain resilience and digital economy integration.
• India reiterated its 'Act East' policy and its vision for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, aligning with ASEAN's Outlook on the Indo-Pacific (AOIP).
• New initiatives were announced in areas of capacity building, counter-terrorism, and cultural exchanges, aimed at deepening strategic partnership.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Progress Report on Foundational Literacy and Numeracy Released
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education today released a comprehensive progress report on the implementation of foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) goals under the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report highlights significant strides in training teachers, developing curriculum frameworks, and distributing learning materials across states, particularly under the NIPUN Bharat Mission.
• Challenges persist in remote areas and among socio-economically disadvantaged groups, requiring targeted interventions and increased resource allocation.
• The government plans to intensify efforts to achieve universal foundational literacy and numeracy by 2027, a key objective of the NEP.
• Emphasis is being placed on leveraging technology for blended learning, continuous assessment, and capacity building for educators.

GS2.headline: Election Commission of India Announces Preparations for Upcoming Assembly Elections: Focus on Digital Voter Services
GS2.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) today held a press conference detailing its preparedness for upcoming Assembly Elections in several states later this year.
• A key focus area is the expansion and enhancement of digital voter services, including online voter registration, correction facilities, and accessible information dissemination through dedicated apps and portals.
• The ECI emphasized measures to ensure free, fair, and transparent elections, including strict enforcement of the Model Code of Conduct and deployment of central forces.
• Special arrangements are being planned to facilitate voting for persons with disabilities (PwDs) and senior citizens, ensuring inclusive participation.
• Training programs for election officials and awareness campaigns for voters are already underway, leveraging technology for wider reach.

GS2.headline: India Pledges Strong Support for Global South Development Initiatives at UN Forum
GS2.explainer:
• India today reiterated its strong commitment to supporting development initiatives for the Global South at a high-level United Nations forum on sustainable development.
• External Affairs Minister emphasized India's role as a voice for developing nations and highlighted its contributions through capacity building, technology transfer, and concessional financing.
• Discussions focused on addressing common challenges such as climate finance, food security, debt sustainability, and achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
• India called for greater equity in global governance structures and stressed the importance of South-South cooperation as a complement to North-South development partnerships.
• The announcement reinforces India's growing influence and leadership in advocating for the collective interests of developing countries on the global stage.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Crosses Major Milestone with Health Records Digitization
GS2.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) today announced a significant milestone, having successfully linked over 500 million health records to Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA).
• This achievement marks a major step towards creating a seamless online platform for healthcare services, enabling citizens to access and share their health records digitally.
• The mission aims to establish a national digital health ecosystem that supports universal health coverage and improves efficiency in healthcare delivery.
• Key components include ABHA ID generation, Health Professional Registry, Health Facility Registry, and a consent-based framework for data sharing.
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare emphasizes the importance of data privacy and security in the digital health infrastructure.

GS2.headline: Women's Reservation Bill: State Assemblies Begin Deliberations on Implementation Modalities
GS2.explainer:
• Following the parliamentary passage of the Women's Reservation Bill (Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam), several state assemblies have commenced deliberations on the modalities of its implementation.
• The bill mandates 33% reservation for women in the Lok Sabha and state legislative assemblies, to be implemented after the next delimitation exercise.
• States are discussing the process of delimitation, potential changes to constituency boundaries, and strategies to ensure fair representation and political empowerment of women.
• Political parties are also strategizing on candidate selection and fostering a more inclusive political environment at the grassroots level.
• The implementation of this landmark legislation is expected to significantly alter India's political landscape, promoting greater gender parity in legislative bodies.

GS2.headline: High Courts Urged to Expedite Cases Related to Environmental Violations and Pollution
GS2.explainer:
• The Chief Justice of India (CJI) today urged High Courts across the country to prioritize and expedite the disposal of cases related to environmental violations and pollution.
• The CJI highlighted the critical need for timely judicial intervention to protect natural resources, enforce environmental laws, and hold polluters accountable.
• Concerns were raised about the backlog of environmental cases, which often leads to irreversible damage before legal remedies can be effectively implemented.
• The call emphasizes the judiciary's role in upholding the 'polluter pays' principle and the 'precautionary principle' in environmental governance.
• Training for judicial officers on complex environmental laws and scientific evidence was also recommended to improve judicial efficiency in this specialized domain.

'Precautionary Principle' advises taking preventive action in the face of uncertainty.
Article 21 (Right to Life) includes the right to a clean environment.

Examine the need for specialized training for judicial officers to handle complex environmental litigation.
Suggest reforms to strengthen environmental governance and ensure timely redressal of environmental grievances in India.
GS2.imageDescription: A courtroom scene with scales of justice overlaid on a background depicting nature and pollution, symbolizing environmental justice.
GS2.trivia: India was one of the first countries to establish an environmental court, the National Green Tribunal, in 2010.

GS2.headline: New Guidelines for Telemedicine Services Released to Expand Healthcare Access in Remote Areas
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare today issued updated guidelines for telemedicine services, aiming to further expand healthcare access, especially in remote and underserved areas.
• The revised framework simplifies regulations for registered medical practitioners, encourages the use of digital platforms, and clarifies ethical considerations for virtual consultations.
• Emphasis is placed on ensuring quality of care, patient safety, and data privacy while leveraging technology to bridge geographical barriers to healthcare.
• The guidelines also promote the integration of telemedicine with the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) to create a comprehensive digital health ecosystem.
• This move is expected to significantly benefit rural populations, reduce the burden on urban healthcare facilities, and enhance the overall efficiency of the health system.

Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

Discuss the key features of the new telemedicine guidelines and their potential impact on healthcare delivery and patient safety.
Examine the challenges in widespread adoption of telemedicine, including digital literacy, infrastructure, and ethical concerns.
Evaluate how telemedicine can be integrated with initiatives like the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission to create a seamless digital health ecosystem.
Suggest policy measures to ensure equitable and effective implementation of telemedicine services across diverse socio-economic groups.

GS2.trivia: The first official telemedicine guidelines in India were released in March 2020 by the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, in collaboration with NITI Aayog.

GS2.headline: Government to Launch New Scheme for Skill Development in Emerging Technologies for Youth Empowerment
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship today announced plans to launch a new scheme aimed at upskilling and reskilling India's youth in emerging technologies.
• The initiative will focus on areas such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine Learning (ML), Data Science, Robotics, and Blockchain, aligning with industry demands.
• Partnerships with industry leaders, educational institutions, and international organizations will be forged to develop relevant curricula and provide hands-on training.
• The scheme seeks to address the skill gap in the rapidly evolving digital economy, enhance employability, and foster an innovation ecosystem.
• Special provisions will be made for vocational training and certification, ensuring that a broad spectrum of youth, including those from economically weaker sections, can benefit.

Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

Aims to bridge the skill gap in the digital economy.
Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) is a flagship skill development scheme.
mainsPoints: Analyze the necessity of skill development in emerging technologies for India's youth to thrive in the 21st-century digital economy.
Discuss the key features of the new government scheme and its potential impact on enhancing employability and fostering innovation.
Examine the challenges in implementing large-scale skill development programs, including infrastructure, trainer availability, and industry-academia linkage.
Evaluate how such initiatives contribute to demographic dividend utilization and inclusive growth.
Suggest strategies for effective collaboration between government, industry, and academia to create a robust skill ecosystem for emerging technologies.
GS2.imageDescription: Young students engaged in a coding workshop, with digital screens displaying lines of code and robotic components.
GS2.trivia: India has one of the largest young populations in the world, often referred to as a 'demographic dividend' if adequately skilled.

GS3.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission: Implementation Roadmap Unveiled, India Aims for Global Leadership
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) today released the detailed implementation roadmap for the National Green Hydrogen Mission, following its significant allocation in the recent Union Budget.
• The roadmap outlines strategies for domestic production, demand creation, export opportunities, and R&D in green hydrogen technologies.
• Key focus areas include developing green hydrogen production hubs, establishing a robust ecosystem for electrolyser manufacturing, and promoting pilot projects in steel, refining, and fertilizer sectors.
• Financial incentives, including production-linked incentives (PLI) and viability gap funding (VGF), are detailed to attract private investment and reduce production costs.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing significantly to energy security and decarbonization goals.

Economic Growth and Development

Science and Technology- Developments and their applications and effects in everyday life

Focus on reducing carbon emissions and achieving energy independence.
Key components include PLI scheme for electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production.

mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the National Green Hydrogen Mission for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the economic potential and challenges in establishing India as a global green hydrogen hub.
Evaluate the role of government policies, including PLI and VGF, in fostering green hydrogen ecosystem.
Examine the interlinkages between the Green Hydrogen Mission and India's commitment to net-zero emissions by 2070.
Critically assess the technological readiness and infrastructure requirements for widespread adoption of green hydrogen.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic depicting green hydrogen production facilities with solar panels and wind turbines in the background.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Inflation Concerns, Signals Cautious Optimism for Growth
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today announced its decision to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive meeting.
• The decision reflects ongoing concerns about persistent retail inflation, particularly in food prices, despite signs of moderation in core inflation.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das emphasized the need for continued vigilance to ensure inflation aligns with the target of 4%, while also acknowledging robust economic growth.
• The MPC maintained its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance, indicating that liquidity management would remain tight to anchor inflationary expectations.
• Experts anticipate the RBI will continue to monitor global commodity prices, monsoon performance, and fiscal policy measures before considering any rate cuts later in the year.

Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy and fiscal policy.

Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets the policy rates.
Primary objective of MPC is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Inflation target for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its implications for inflation and economic growth.
Discuss the challenges faced by the Monetary Policy Committee in balancing inflation control with supporting economic activity.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance in managing liquidity and inflationary pressures.
Examine the external and internal factors influencing RBI's monetary policy decisions, including global commodity prices and domestic demand.
Critically assess the coordination between monetary and fiscal policy in achieving India's macroeconomic stability goals.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building with its logo prominently displayed.
GS3.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, in accordance with the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

GS3.headline: Project Tiger Completes 50 Years: New Conservation Strategies for Big Cats Unveiled
GS3.explainer:
• Celebrating 50 years of Project Tiger, the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) today announced new strategies for tiger conservation and broader big cat protection.
• The new approach emphasizes community involvement, use of advanced technology for surveillance, and strengthening anti-poaching measures across tiger reserves.
• Focus will also be on habitat restoration, managing human-wildlife conflict, and creating corridors for tiger movement to ensure genetic diversity.
• The initiative will extend its scope to other big cats like leopards and snow leopards, recognizing their ecological importance and shared threats.
• India's tiger population has significantly increased since Project Tiger's inception in 1973, positioning the country as a global leader in tiger conservation.

Biodiversity and its conservation.

National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) is the statutory body for tiger conservation.

Global Tiger Day is celebrated on July 29.

Discuss the new strategies announced for big cat conservation, including community involvement and technological interventions.
Analyze the challenges posed by human-wildlife conflict and habitat fragmentation to tiger conservation efforts.
Examine the role of international cooperation and national policies in achieving conservation goals for endangered species.
Suggest comprehensive measures to ensure the long-term sustainability of big cat populations in India amidst developmental pressures.
GS3.imageDescription: A majestic Bengal tiger walking through dense forest foliage in a tiger reserve.
GS3.trivia: India's tiger population has more than quadrupled since the launch of Project Tiger, making it one of the most successful conservation programs globally.

GS3.headline: ISRO Prepares for Venus Orbiter Mission 'Shukrayaan-1' Launch Window
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today confirmed that preparations are in full swing for the launch of its ambitious Venus Orbiter Mission, 'Shukrayaan-1', targeting a launch window in late 2026 or early 2027.
• Shukrayaan-1 aims to study Venus's atmosphere, surface, and subsurface, providing crucial insights into the planet's geological and atmospheric evolution.
• The mission payload includes a high-resolution synthetic aperture radar (SAR) to map the Venusian surface through its thick clouds, and instruments to study atmospheric composition and dynamics.
• This mission marks India's first foray into exploring Venus, building on the success of Chandrayaan and Mangalyaan missions.
• International collaborations are also being explored for data sharing and scientific analysis, enhancing global understanding of Earth's 'sister planet'.

Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: Shukrayaan-1 is India's first mission to Venus.

Payloads include SAR for surface mapping.
Venus is Earth's 'sister planet' due to similar size and mass.
mainsPoints: Discuss the scientific objectives and potential discoveries of ISRO's Shukrayaan-1 mission to Venus.
Analyze the significance of India's planetary exploration efforts for its scientific capabilities and global standing.
Examine the technological challenges involved in designing and executing a mission to Venus, given its extreme environment.
Evaluate the benefits of international collaboration in space exploration, citing examples from past missions.
How do missions like Shukrayaan-1 contribute to our understanding of Earth's climate and future habitability?
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of the Shukrayaan-1 orbiter approaching Venus, with its scientific instruments deployed.
GS3.trivia: Venus is the hottest planet in our solar system, even though Mercury is closer to the Sun, due to its thick atmosphere of greenhouse gases.

GS3.headline: India Enhances Border Infrastructure along Northern Frontier with Advanced Surveillance Systems
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence today announced significant advancements in border infrastructure development and deployment of advanced surveillance systems along India's northern frontier.
• The upgrade includes construction of new roads, bridges, and tunnels to ensure all-weather connectivity for troops and equipment in challenging terrains.
• Integrated surveillance systems, comprising drones, satellite imagery, and ground-based sensors, are being operationalized to enhance situational awareness and deter infiltration.
• This strategic push aims to bolster India's defensive capabilities and ensure rapid response to any security challenges in the region.
• The move is part of a broader strategy to modernize border management and integrate technology for effective monitoring and protection of national borders.

Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: India shares land borders with seven countries.
Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is responsible for building and maintaining border roads.
Use of drones and satellite imagery for border surveillance.
Integrated border management aims for comprehensive security.

Examine the challenges faced in constructing infrastructure in high-altitude and rugged terrains.
Evaluate the impact of improved border infrastructure on national security and regional stability.

GS3.imageDescription: Military personnel patrolling a newly constructed road in a mountainous border region, with surveillance equipment in the background.
GS3.trivia: The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) was formed in 1960 and has been instrumental in developing and maintaining road networks in India's border areas.

GS3.headline: Government Launches New PLI Scheme for Advanced Battery Manufacturing to Boost EV Ecosystem
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Heavy Industries today announced the launch of a new Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme specifically targeting Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) battery manufacturing in India.
• The scheme aims to attract significant domestic and foreign investment, reduce import dependence, and establish India as a global manufacturing hub for EV batteries.
• Incentives will be provided to manufacturers based on their output and domestic value addition, encouraging local production of critical components and raw materials.
• This initiative is crucial for accelerating the adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) and achieving India's ambitious climate goals.
• The scheme is expected to foster R&D in battery technology, create employment opportunities, and enhance India's energy storage capabilities.

GS3.trivia: The global demand for electric vehicle batteries is projected to grow significantly, with lithium-ion batteries currently dominating the market.

GS3.headline: Government to Boost Millets Production and Export under 'Shree Anna' Initiative
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare today announced enhanced measures to boost the production and export of millets, branded as 'Shree Anna', following the emphasis in the recent Union Budget.
• The initiative aims to promote millets as a superfood, recognizing their nutritional benefits and resilience to climate change, especially for small and marginal farmers.
• Plans include providing financial assistance to farmers for millet cultivation, improving post-harvest infrastructure, and supporting value addition and branding efforts.
• India, being a major producer of millets, seeks to become a global hub for 'Shree Anna' through aggressive marketing and export promotion.
• This push aligns with the International Year of Millets (2023) and India's commitment to food security and sustainable agriculture.

GS3.trivia: Millets are ancient grains, cultivated for thousands of years, and are known for their ability to grow in arid and semi-arid regions with minimal water.

GS3.headline: India's Tourism Sector Sees Robust Growth Post-Pandemic, Focus on Sustainable and Experiential Tourism
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism today released data indicating a robust recovery and growth in India's tourism sector, surpassing pre-pandemic levels in both domestic and international arrivals.
• The resurgence is attributed to government initiatives like 'Dekho Apna Desh' and 'Swadesh Darshan', coupled with improved infrastructure and enhanced connectivity.
• A new strategy focuses on promoting sustainable tourism practices, responsible travel, and developing unique experiential tourism products, including eco-tourism and cultural circuits.
• Efforts are underway to leverage digital platforms for promotion and booking, enhancing the visitor experience and supporting local economies.
• The sector is poised for continued growth, contributing significantly to employment generation and foreign exchange earnings.

GS3.headline: New Species of Fauna Discovered in Western Ghats, Highlights Biodiversity Richness and Conservation Needs
GS3.explainer:
• Scientists from the Zoological Survey of India (ZSI) today announced the discovery of several new species of amphibians and insects in the biodiverse Western Ghats region.
• The findings underscore the immense biodiversity richness of the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the world's eight 'hottest' biodiversity hotspots.
• These discoveries highlight the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts, protection of critical habitats, and sustainable development practices in the region.
• Researchers emphasized the importance of continued exploration and documentation of species to better understand and preserve India's natural heritage.
• The new species will undergo detailed taxonomic classification and genetic analysis, contributing valuable data to global biodiversity inventories.

GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas, and their forests influence the Indian monsoon weather patterns.
